title: Witheringia laxissima
intro: Witheringia laxissima, also known as Witheringia, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Solanaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including the treatment of fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Witheringia include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Witheringia is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into tinctures and capsules for the treatment of various health conditions.
